subject: Physics
title: The Casimir Effect
description: A 60-second lesson on The Casimir Effect.

hook.text: Place two metal plates extremely close together in a vacuum and they attract—pushed by nothing visible.

buildup.text: Quantum mechanics says empty space isn't empty. Virtual particles pop in and out of existence constantly.

discovery.text: Between the plates, only some wavelengths of virtual particles fit. Outside, all exist. The imbalance creates force.

twist.text: Hendrik Casimir predicted this in 1948. It was confirmed experimentally in 1997 with plates just micrometers apart.

climax.text: The Casimir effect proves that empty space has energy—vacuum energy is real and measurable.

punchline.text: Nothing pushes the plates together. Except nothing isn't empty.

quiz.question: What causes the Casimir effect?
options: An imbalance of virtual particle wavelengths between and outside the plates
Static electricity between the metal surfaces
Magnetic attraction from the metal plates
